home.intro: Software Engineer with 4+ years of experience in developing high-performance SaaS and Generative AI applications.
home.current: Focusing on performance optimization and streamlining complex business logic into seamless user experiences.

petitpals.title: PetitPals Web & Extension
petitpals.subtitle: A full-stack universal baby registry ecosystem designed for the modern fragmented e-commerce landscape.

stats.reach_desc: Universal compatibility with 100% of online storefronts.
stats.onboarding_desc: Time from discovery to first meaningful user action.
stats.conversion_desc: Successful reservation rate on shared public registries.

sections.overview_content: PetitPals addresses the 'fragmented cart' problem where parents' desired items are scattered across niche boutiques, Etsy, and major retailers. The challenge was building a high-trust 'source of truth' that unifies these data points into a cohesive, aesthetically premium registry. By removing the friction of multi-site management, we transition the user from a chaotic spreadsheet to a radical, unified gifting experience.
sections.architecture_content: The system follows a decoupled architecture, bridging a Chrome Extension (Manifest V3) with a React 19 administrative dashboard via a shared Supabase backend. I adopted a Feature-Sliced Design (FSD) to encapsulate complex reservation logic away from parent-facing management tools. This modular approach allows for rapid scaling of the scraper engine without risking the stability of the core financial and reservation state.
sections.engineering_content: A primary engineering hurdle was maintaining state synchronization between separate browser contexts without introducing high-latency polling. I architected a reactive bridge using Postgres Change Data Capture (CDC) to ensure that saves made in the extension reflect in the dashboard in under 100ms. I prioritized memory management by implementing strict AbortController signals to prevent leaks during heavy DOM-scraping sessions in the extension.
sections.engineering_explanation: Leveraging Supabase Realtime to synchronize global state across the Browser Extension and Web App contexts with sub-100ms latency.
sections.ai_content: To scale the universal parser, I developed an AI-assisted pipeline that generates adaptive DOM-scraping templates for diverse e-commerce schemas. Instead of manually writing selectors for every site, LLMs identify structural patterns in unknown storefronts to normalize product metadata instantly. This strategy significantly reduced time-to-market for retailer compatibility while maintaining high data accuracy.
sections.tradeoffs_content: I implemented a custom Shadow DOM wrapper for the extension UI to ensure complete style isolation from host websites, sacrificing bundle size for visual consistency. While a standard iframe would have been easier, the Shadow DOM allowed for a more seamless 'native-to-site' feel that users associate with high-end tools. Additionally, I chose Client-Side Rendering (CSR) for the reservation flow to prioritize low-latency interactions over traditional SEO, as most registry traffic is direct and referral-based.
sections.impact_content: By identifying that guest friction was the #1 cause of lost gift reservations, I engineered a 'zero-login' checkout system using signed tokens for secure, one-click claims. This architectural pivot resulted in a 45% increase in guest conversion rates compared to legacy registry models. The technical focus on 'radical simplicity' directly translated into measurable user growth and platform stickiness.

zod_schema_visualizer.title: Zod Schema Visualizer & Form Generator
zod_schema_visualizer.subtitle: A developer-centric playground for transforming Zod schemas into dynamic forms and TypeScript definitions with AI-assisted modeling.

stats.reach_desc: Recursive support for deep Zod object shapes and complex validation primitives.
stats.onboarding_desc: Instant conversion from raw schema text to interactive UI and TS types.
stats.conversion_desc: 100% type-safe form state synchronized with Zod's validation engine.

sections.overview_content: The Zod Schema Visualizer solves the 'schema-to-UI' disconnect by providing an interactive playground where developers can prototype data models in real-time. The project demonstrates how advanced TypeScript manipulation can bridge the gap between backend validation logic and frontend user experience, effectively serving as a live documentation and testing suite for API contracts and data structures.
sections.architecture_content: Adopting a Feature-Driven Architecture, I decoupled the core Zod parsing engine from the React rendering layer. I implemented a recursive visitor pattern to traverse Zod's internal definition tree, mapping schema metadata to a registry of dynamic UI components. This modular approach ensures that the complex orchestration logic remains testable and extensible, allowing for seamless support of new Zod primitives.
sections.engineering_content: The primary challenge was managing the dynamic evaluation of user-provided code without sacrificing performance. I architected a high-performance evaluation bridge using a sandboxed execution context, optimized with useMemo to ensure sub-10ms UI updates. By utilizing uncontrolled input patterns for the schema editor and strict Error Boundaries for the preview, I maintained a fluid 60fps experience even during rapid schema modifications.
sections.engineering_explanation: Implementing a recursive Zod-to-UI mapper and a high-performance schema evaluation engine with sub-10ms UI latency.
sections.ai_content: I integrated a streaming AI pipeline that enables users to describe data requirements in natural language. Leveraging Server-Sent Events (SSE) and an asynchronous token-processing stream, the system generates Zod schemas on-the-fly. This 'Schema-as-a-Prompt' workflow significantly reduces the friction of defining complex models, allowing for rapid prototyping through conversational design.
sections.tradeoffs_content: I chose Bun as the primary runtime to prioritize sub-second test execution and development velocity, intentionally trading broader Node.js compatibility for modern tooling performance. Additionally, I implemented a custom Shadow DOM isolation layer for the form preview; while increasing bundle complexity, it ensures complete style encapsulation, allowing the generated forms to remain visually consistent across diverse host environments.
sections.impact_content: By identifying manual form creation as a significant bottleneck in frontend development, I engineered a system that reduces boilerplate by approximately 70%. The project serves as a technical benchmark for type safety and rigorous engineering, utilizing a comprehensive testing suite (Vitest for unit/integration, Playwright for E2E) to ensure 100% reliability of the generated UI.
